---
title: "Strategic Qualitative Principles in Practice"
description: "Study note on Strategic Qualitative Principles in Practice in Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods."
book_id: "qualitative-research-evaluation-methods"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "140-141"
citation_key: "patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod08-module-8-integrating-the-12-strategic-qualitative-principles-in-practice-chapter:c0010"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 140-141</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Strategic Qualitative Principles in Practice" from Michael Quinn Patton's *Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods* [@patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The section frames qualitative inquiry as a set of strategic principles that must be adapted to practical field conditions.

## Study Summary

The section frames qualitative inquiry as a set of strategic principles that must be adapted to practical field conditions.

It rejects the idea that qualitative methods only belong in ideal situations; their value often appears when evaluation questions require context-sensitive judgment.

The evaluator's task is to maintain qualitative discipline while adjusting to real constraints in access, time, and use.

## Key Concepts

- Strategic principles
- Field adaptation
- Qualitative rigor
- Practical constraints

## Source Location

- Section path: Part 1 > Chapter 2: Strategic Themes in Qualitative Inquiry > Module 8
- Evidence records: `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod08-module-8-integrating-the-12-strategic-qualitative-principles-in-practice-chapter:c0010`

## Practical Implications

- Define what qualitative evidence is expected to clarify before collecting data.
- Plan for adaptation while protecting documentation and analytic transparency.
- Use qualitative design choices to make trade-offs explicit.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Strategic Qualitative Principles in Practice helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
